slug: deaf-spatial-design
query: "Deaf signing space corridor width sightlines visual communication built environment spatial design"
last_searched: 2026-03-19 01:05

  NO: tegnrom / kommunikasjonsavstand [PARTIAL — no standard term; concept emerging]
  DA: tegnsprogrum / kommunikationsafstand [CLEAN]
  FI: viittomistila / visuaalinen kommunikointitila [CLEAN]
  FR: espace de signe / espace proxémique pour la LSF [CLEAN]
  DE: Gebärdenraum / Kommunikationszone [CLEAN]
  ZH: 手语交流空间 (shǒuyǔ jiāoliú kōngjiān) [PARTIAL — no standard built-environment term in ZH standards]
  JA: 手話コミュニケーションスペース / 手話空間 [PARTIAL — field underdeveloped in JA built-environment standards]
  NL: gebarenruimte / visuele communicatieruimte [CLEAN]
  ES: espacio para la lengua de signos / espacio signante [CLEAN]
  PT: espaço para língua de sinais / espaço signante [CLEAN]
  KO: 수어 공간 (sueo gonggan) [PARTIAL — no standard built-environment term; field nascent]
  IT: spazio per la lingua dei segni / spazio segnante [CLEAN]

concept_boundary_warnings:
  - NO: No standard Norwegian built-environment term for signing space; TEK17 does not address DEAF spatial requirements; search via Norges Døveforbund advocacy materials
  - ZH: Signing space as a built-environment design category does not exist in Chinese standards (GB 50763-2012, GB 55019-2021); JFD equivalent (中国聋人协会) does not publish built-environment guidance; concept mapped from advocacy and academic literature only
  - JA: バリアフリー法 does not address Deaf spatial requirements beyond hearing loop provision; ろう学校 (Deaf school) design guidelines (MEXT) are the nearest relevant source; JFD (全日本ろうあ連盟) focuses on social welfare not built environment
  - KO: 편의증진법 addresses hearing loop provision only; 한국농아인협회 does not publish built-environment spatial guidance; field entirely nascent
  - DE: DIN 18040-1/2 does not address signing space; Deutscher Gehörlosen-Bund (DGB) guidance is for digital/media contexts; no German built-environment signing space standard exists; Bundesfachstelle Barrierefreiheit material is communication-access focused not spatial
  - IT: DM 236/89 and DPR 380/2001 do not address LIS/signing space; ENS Osservatorio Accessibilità focuses on communication barriers; Italian built-environment signing space guidance absent from standards

concept_boundary_warnings:
  - ZH: GB 50763 addresses hearing loop provision but RT60 targets for hearing-impaired not specified; Chinese research uses 混响时间 (hùnxiǎng shíjiān) for reverberation time; CI acoustic environment research published in Chinese rehabilitation medicine journals not built-environment databases
  - JA: バリアフリー法 specifies hearing loop (ヒアリングループ) provision but not RT60 targets; Japanese CI acoustic research predominantly in audiology not built environment; MLIT does not specify acoustic performance targets for DEAF population
